title: prunus × pollardii
intro: Prunus × pollardii, a hybrid cherry tree, is a cross between Prunus avium (wild cherry) and Prunus serotina (black cherry), resulting in a unique and valuable plant with various medicinal properties. The bark and leaves of Prunus × pollardii contain salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, which has anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects. The medicinal properties of Prunus × pollardii make it beneficial for treating headaches, fever, and rheumatism, while the health benefits of consuming its fruit, leaves, or bark include antioxidant and antimicrobial properties. Prunus × pollardii can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being used in traditional medicine to promote digestive health and reduce pain and inflammation.
